task: I’m putting together a simple backup plan in case I’m away from home for a day or two and need to keep the usual things covered. What are the main items worth thinking through first so I can keep it practical and not forget anything important?

turns.agent_response:
A practical way to think about it is to cover the **few things that matter most if you’re gone 1–2 days**:

### 1) Access
- Who can get in if needed
- Spare key location
- Any alarm codes, gate codes, or entry instructions

### 2) Utilities and safety
- Main water shutoff
- Breaker panel location
- Gas shutoff if relevant
- Smoke/CO alarm status

### 3) Health and emergencies
- Doctor and pharmacy contact info
- Emergency contact numbers
- Any urgent medical notes if someone may need to help

### 4) What “normal” looks like at home
- Usual routine or anything that might seem unusual but is normal
- Pets, plants, timers, lights, or appliances that should stay as-is

### 5) Security
- Make it look occupied
- Hold mail/packages
- Lights/timers
- Close blinds/curtains in a normal way
- Don’t leave obvious signs that nobody’s home

### 6) Quick written backup note
Keep it short and easy to find, with:
- Full name and number of the person who has access
- Where the spare key is
- Shutoff valves and breaker panel location
- Doctor, pharmacy, and emergency contact numbers if you’re away more than a day
- Short note on what is normal in the apartment

If you want, I can turn this into a **one-page fill-in template** you can keep on your phone or print out.

user_simulator.text:
Mostly right. I’d want it tighter, though — and a little more specific about **who knows what**.

For me the first pass is:
- access, yes
- utilities/shutoffs, yes
- emergency contacts and meds, yes
- what’s normal in the place, yes
- mail/packages, yes

But I’d also want:
- **one actual person** named, not a vague “contact”
- whether that person is allowed to enter or just call
- where the spare key is kept, if there is one
- any regular bills or deliveries that might show up while I’m gone
- a short note on **what not to do** if something looks off

I don’t need a long explanation. A one-page template would be fine, but make it plain and not cutesy.

turns.agent_response:
Here’s a tighter first-pass list:

### Backup plan basics
- **One named person**
  - Name
  - Phone number
  - What they’re allowed to do: **enter / call only / handle emergencies only**

- **Access**
  - Spare key location, if there is one
  - Door code, gate code, alarm code, if needed

- **Utilities and shutoffs**
  - Water shutoff
  - Gas shutoff, if applicable
  - Breaker panel
  - Any other main controls

- **Emergency info**
  - Emergency contact
  - Doctor
  - Pharmacy
  - Medications that matter

- **What’s normal**
  - Pets, plants, lights, timers, appliances, routines

- **Mail, packages, bills, deliveries**
  - Anything expected while you’re gone
  - What to do with it

- **What not to do**
  - Don’t force entry
  - Don’t reset anything unless instructed
  - Don’t move or discard anything if it looks unusual
  - Call first if something seems off

If you want, I can turn that into a **plain one-page fill-in template**.

user_simulator.text:
Better. Still want the template, not just the outline. Plain, one page, with blanks I can fill in.

And keep the wording practical — no “backup plan basics,” no fluff. I’d want sections for:
- named person and what they’re allowed to do
- access details
- shutoffs
- meds / doctor / pharmacy
- normal routine
- mail, packages, bills, deliveries
- what not to do

If you’re doing the template, include a spot for a second person too, in case the first one can’t be reached. One page only.
That’s the thing I wanted. Plain enough, and the second person box is right. Fine as-is.
